title: "modular energy storage and dispensing system"
abstract: Some embodiments relate to a modular hydrogen storage and dispensing system. An example system includes: a hydrogen storage subsystem, including a storage frame and a hydrogen storage vessel configured to fit within the storage frame; a hydrogen dispensing subsystem, configured to dispense hydrogen from the storage vessel through a dispensing outlet; a safety subsystem, including at least one isolation valve installed between the storage subsystem and the dispensing subsystem, and an externally accessible emergency cut-off actuator to cut off power to non-safety equipment and to cause each isolation valve to adopt a fail-safe position; and a control subsystem, configured to control the storage subsystem, the dispensing subsystem, and the safety subsystem. The safety subsystem further includes a leak detection system comprising at least one hydrogen sensor disposed within the storage frame and configured to transmit a cut off signal to cut power to non-safety equipment and to cause each isolation valve to adopt the fail-safe position when a leak is detected.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1. A modular hydrogen storage and dispensing system, including: a hydrogen storage subsystem, including: a storage frame having external dimensions of a standard ISO shipping container; and a pressurised unitary hydrogen storage vessel, configured to fit within the storage frame; a hydrogen dispensing subsystem, configured to dispense hydrogen from the hydrogen storage vessel through a dispensing outlet; a safety subsystem, including at least one isolation valve installed between the hydrogen storage subsystem and the hydrogen dispensing subsystem, and an externally accessible emergency cut-off actuator to cut off power to non-safety equipment and to cause each isolation valve to adopt a fail-safe position, wherein the safety subsystem further includes a leak detection system comprising at least one hydrogen sensor disposed within the storage frame and configured to transmit a cut off signal to cut power to non-safety equipment and to cause each isolation valve to adopt the fail-safe position when a leak is detected; and a control subsystem, configured to control the hydrogen storage subsystem, the hydrogen dispensing subsystem, and the safety subsystem. 2. The system of claim 1, wherein the storage subsystem is configured to store gaseous hydrogen within the pressurised storage vessel. 3. The system of any one of claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the dispensing subsystem is configured to dispense gaseous hydrogen. 4. The system of any one of claim 2 or claim 3, wherein the safety subsystem includes an emergency venting system, configured to vent excess gaseous hydrogen from the storage vessel into the surrounding atmosphere. 5. The system of claim 1, wherein the storage subsystem is configured to store liquid hydrogen within the pressurised storage vessel and includes a cryogenic cooling system to maintain the liquid hydrogen at a safe temperature. 6. The system of any one of claim 1 or claim 5, wherein the dispensing subsystem is configured to convert liquid hydrogen to gaseous hydrogen, and to then dispense gaseous hydrogen. 7. The system of any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein the storage vessel comprises: a primary inner shell to contain the hydrogen; and a secondary outer shell to encapsulate the primary shell and to act as a secondary barrier in the event of a rapture in the primary shell. 8. The system of claim 7, wherein an interstitial space between the primary shell and the secondary shell is negatively pressurised to allow leak detection using vacuum sensors, and to provide thermal insulation. 9. The system of claim 7, wherein an interstitial space between the primary shell and the secondary shell contains a fire-retardant mixture to provide fire protection to the storage vessel and to provide thermal insulation. 10. The system of any one of claims 1 to 9, wherein the dispensing subsystem includes a metering device configured to accurately track mass transfer of hydrogen into and out of the hydrogen storage vessel. 11. The system of any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein the modular hydrogen storage and dispensing subsystem further includes a transfer pump to facilitate the 

transfer of hydrogen from a transport unit into the storage vessel within the storage subsystem via a hose attached to a high-pressure coupler attached to the storage subsystem. 12. The system of any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein the modular hydrogen storage and dispensing subsystem is configured to receive hydrogen from a transport unit, wherein the transport unit carries a transfer pump to facilitate the transfer of hydrogen from a transport unit into the storage vessel within the storage subsystem via a hose attached to a high-pressure coupler attached to the storage subsystem. 13. The system of any one of claims 1 to 12, wherein the storage frame has external dimensions of any one of a 20-foot general purpose ISO shipping container, a 20-foot high cube ISO shipping container, a 40-foot general purpose ISO shipping container, or a 40-foot high cube ISO shipping container. 14. The system of any one of claims 1 to 13, further including a battery module configured to store electrical energy. 15. The system of any one of claims 1 to 14, further including a solar module, comprising a number of solar panels configured to convert solar energy into electrical energy. 16. The system of any one of claims 1 to 15, further including a wind turbine module, comprising a number of wind turbines configured to convert wind energy into electrical energy. 17. The system of any one of claim 15 or claim 16, wherein the solar and/or wind modules are supported by a canopy coupled to the storage frame, the canopy supporting a number of solar panels and/or wind turbines configured to produce electrical power for the modular hydrogen storage and dispensing subsystem. 18. The system of any one of claims 1 to 17, further including an electric vehicle charging module, configured to provide suitable vehicles with electrical power. 19. The system of any one of claims 1 to 18, further including a hydrogen fuel cell power generation module, configured to convert hydrogen from the hydrogen storage vessel into electrical energy. 20. The system of any one of claims 1 to 19, further including a hydrogen gas generation and liquefying module, configured to convert stored water into either gaseous or liquid hydrogen, and then store the hydrogen in the storage vessel within the storage subsystem. 21. The system of any one of claims 1 to 20, wherein the control subsystem is further configured to log hydrogen inflow data, hydrogen outflow data, and hydrogen storage level data. 22. The system of any one of claims 1 to 21, wherein the control subsystem is further configured to monitor operation of the modular hydrogen storage and dispensing subsystem and to log diagnostic data based on the monitored operation. 23. The system of claim 22, wherein the control subsystem is further configured to execute code modules to analyse, quantify, and interpret the logged diagnostic data to generate reports, and to electronically transmit the reports to an external server.
